text: Argo just got six new features. Scene transitions, speed ramp, GIF export, batch pipeline, a multi-demo dashboard, and an export progress bar. Let's take a look.

overlay.title: Six new features in Argo
overlay.body: Transitions, speed ramp, GIF, batch, dashboard, progress bar.

text: Here they all are at a glance. Scene transitions for smooth visual breaks. Speed ramp to compress boring gaps. GIF export for docs and READMEs. Batch pipeline to build all demos at once. A dashboard to see everything. And a progress bar so you know when encoding will finish.

overlay.text: Six features — all config-driven, no API keys needed

text: Scene transitions add smooth visual breaks between scenes. Fade through black is the classic film cut. Dissolve does a quick opacity crossfade. And wipe slides the next scene in from the left or right. One line of config, four options.

Speed ramp compresses the boring parts. Navigation, page loads, waiting for animations — all sped up. Key interactions stay at normal speed. Set gap speed to two and your thirty-second demo becomes twenty-two.

overlay.title: Speed Ramp
overlay.body: Gaps play at 2× — scenes stay at full speed.

text: Multi-format export gives you one pipeline run, multiple outputs. Square for Instagram and LinkedIn. Vertical for TikTok and Reels. And animated GIF with palette optimization for docs and pull request previews.

Batch pipeline discovers every demo in your project and builds them all. Just run argo pipeline dash-dash all. If one demo fails, the rest keep going. And the dashboard shows every demo at a glance — build status, file sizes, resolution.

overlay.text: argo pipeline --all — build everything at once

text: The export progress bar shows real-time encoding progress. No more staring at a silent terminal wondering if ffmpeg is still running. You get a live percentage bar that updates as frames are encoded.
All six features are available today. Update your config, run the pipeline, and you're set. And yes — this demo used scene transitions and speed ramp to record itself.

overlay.title: npm i -D @argo-video/cli
overlay.body: Update your config and re-run: npx argo pipeline --all
